evidence: The set B is defined by forbidding residues: m not congruent to x (mod n) for all x in X_n, a subset of Z/nZ chosen for each n.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research open]; QID live, label exact; B is defined by residue exclusion in ZMod n - modular arithmetic is load-bearing

evidence: The question asks whether B must have a logarithmic density (HasLogDensity), the log-weighted variant of natural/asymptotic density.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; question is HasLogDensity - logarithmic density is a variant covered under the natural-density umbrella (same enwiki article family) and the density notion is the whole question

evidence: Docstring: when A = {p^2 : p prime}, B is the set of squarefree numbers, and Erdős proved the gap-sum limit exists in this case.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research solved]; QID live; the variant's set B is literally the squarefree integers

evidence: The multiset A_k of all sums of k distinct elements of A (sumMultiset via powersetCard) is the restricted-sumset data determining A in the Selfridge-Straus problem.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research solved]; QID live; sums of k distinct elements of A = k-fold restricted sumset, the data that determines A in Selfridge-Straus

evidence: Docstring states 'This is also known as the Littlewood conjecture' and the decl states liminf n ||n alpha|| ||n beta|| = 0 for all real alpha, beta.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research open]; QID live; statement is exactly liminf n||na||||nb||=0 for all real a,b - this IS the Littlewood conjecture (docstring confirms)

evidence: The statement bounds n * distToNearestInt(n*alpha) * distToNearestInt(n*beta), the central quantity of simultaneous Diophantine approximation; erdosproblems.com tags it 'diophantine approximation'.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; ||na|| (distToNearestInt) is the central object; the conjecture is a core simultaneous-Diophantine-approximation statement, not tangential

evidence: The statement quantifies over all real n x n doubly stochastic matrices (M in doublyStochastic R (Fin n)) and asks for a permutation diagonal with product at least n^-n.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research solved]; QID live; quantifies over M in doublyStochastic R (Fin n)

evidence: The van der Waerden conjecture decl bounds M.permanent below by n^-n * n! for doubly stochastic M.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research solved]; QID live; conclusion bounds M.permanent

evidence: Docstring: 'the permanent of a doubly stochastic matrix is at least n^-n n!', proved by Gyires, Egorychev, and Falikman.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; van der Waerden statement is about doubly stochastic matrices explicitly

evidence: The problem concerns the asymptotic distribution function of phi(n)/n (IsDistributionOfPhiRatio uses Nat.totient) and whether its derivative is ever positive.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research open]; QID live (alias 'totient function' + label match); statement is about phi(n)/n via Nat.totient

evidence: IsDistributionOfPhiRatio defines f(c) as the natural density of {n : phi(n) < cn} via Set.HasDensity.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; IsDistributionOfPhiRatio is defined via Set.HasDensity = natural density

evidence: Docstring: Erdős [Er95] proved the distribution function of phi(n)/n is purely singular - continuous with derivative zero almost everywhere (IsPurelySingular).
verify_note: decl exists @[category research solved]; QID live; IsPurelySingular (continuous, deriv 0 a.e.) is exactly a singular function

evidence: The hypothesis is that each bounded set A_x has Lebesgue outer measure < 1 (volume.toOuterMeasure (A x) < 1).
verify_note: decl exists @[category research open]; QID live; hypothesis volume.toOuterMeasure (A x) < 1 makes outer measure load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: Hechler [He72] showed the answer is no assuming the continuum hypothesis; the decl hypothesizes aleph_1 = continuum.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research solved]; QID live; decl hypothesizes aleph_1 = continuum, i.e. CH
